title: cli.py: extract-text command
description: Wire `pypptx extract-text <file.pptx> [--slides 1,3,5] [--output file.txt]` into `cli.py`. Parse the `--slides` comma-separated list into a `list[int]`. Without `--output`, write plain text directly to stdout (bypassing `output_result()`). With `--output`, write text to the file and output JSON `{"output_file": "...", "slide_count": N}` to stdout.
acceptance_criteria: `pypptx extract-text deck.pptx` writes plain text with `--- Slide N ---` delimiters to stdout regardless of `--plain`.
`pypptx extract-text deck.pptx --slides 1,3` outputs only slides 1 and 3.
`pypptx extract-text deck.pptx --output out.txt` writes text to `out.txt` and outputs `{"output_file": "out.txt", "slide_count": N}` to stdout.
`--plain` with `--output` outputs just the filename to stdout.
Invalid slide indices (out of range, non-integer) go to stderr; exit code is non-zero.
Errors go to stderr; exit code is non-zero on failure.

handoff_summary.completed: Wired `extract-text` CLI command into `cli.py`: argument parsing, slide filter validation, stdout plain-text output (no `--output`), file write + JSON result output (with `--output`), and `--plain` passthrough to `output_result()`.
handoff_summary.risks: slide_count is derived by counting `--- Slide ` delimiter lines in the extracted text; this is correct but depends on the delimiter format staying stable in `ops/extract.py`.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should validate all six acceptance criteria against a real .pptx fixture.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts expected; only `pypptx/cli.py` was modified and no sibling beads claim that file.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: slide_count counted from delimiter lines in extracted text rather than re-opening the Presentation a second time, avoiding a duplicate I/O round-trip. Presentation is only opened once (for index validation when --slides is supplied).
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tests needed: stdout plain text (no --output), --slides filter, --output file write + JSON stdout, --plain + --output filename-only stdout, non-integer slide index error, out-of-range slide index error, unreadable file error.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Out-of-range validation requires loading the Presentation even before extract_text is called, which means the file is parsed twice when --slides is provided.

conflict_risks: No conflicts expected; only `pypptx/cli.py` was modified and no sibling beads claim that file.

execution_history.summary: Implemented `extract-text` command in `pypptx/cli.py`. The command accepts a `.pptx` file argument with optional `--slides` (comma-separated 1-based indices), `--output` (file path), and `--plain` flags. Without `--output`, plain text is written directly to stdout. With `--output`, text is written to the file and JSON `{"output_file": "...", "slide_count": N}` (or just the filename with `--plain`) is written to stdout. Non-integer or out-of-range slide indices produce an error message on stderr with a non-zero exit code. Also added the missing `from pathlib import Path` import.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
